title: Keystone Reference and Senate Finance Questions
summary: Drop #7 introduced the term 'Keystone' and referenced Senate financial oversight themes. The term was never clearly defined by Q.
raw_text:
What is Keystone?
Why is this relevant?
Who controls the Senate?
Who controls the finance committees?
What happens if corruption is exposed?
Q

lines.fact: The term was not defined by Q and has no universally accepted interpretation within the drop archive.
lines.theory: Followers speculated it referred to financial systems, intelligence coordination, or hidden infrastructure.
lines.context: Ambiguous keywords became common participation mechanisms in Q communities.
lines.questions: Did Q ever directly clarify the meaning of Keystone in later drops?

lines.fact: Congressional finance-related committees are formally controlled by elected members and party leadership structures.
lines.theory: Q followers interpreted the line as implying hidden financial influence over legislative priorities.
lines.context: Committee leadership often shapes taxation, banking, and oversight policy.
lines.questions: What documented evidence exists of improper financial influence over committee operations?
